description_long: This mushroom object has a rounded, irregularly lumpy cap with a predominantly beige surface, accented by large, patchy areas of rusty red coloration. The cap texture appears slightly rough and uneven, with subtle surface ridges and shallow depressions. The coloration is not uniform, with the red patches concentrated more heavily on one side, giving it a weathered, natural look. There is no visible stem, and the underside of the cap is not shown, making it difficult to determine the presence of gills. The object appears solid and dense, resembling a wild mushroom that might be found growing on soil or a rocky surface in an urban park. Its overall form is squat and compact, with a cap that merges directly into the base.
description: A squat, rounded mushroom with a rough beige cap and large rusty red patches, lacking a visible stem. The surface is irregular and naturalistic, resembling a wild mushroom.
description_view_0: The cap appears mostly beige with subtle red patches and a rough, lumpy texture.
description_view_1: This side shows a prominent rusty red area covering much of the cap, with the beige base still visible.
description_view_2: The cap is dominated by a large, continuous rusty red patch, with only small areas of beige showing through.
description_view_3: Red patches are prominent but more dispersed, with beige areas and surface ridges visible.
attribute_cap_pattern: patchy, rough, with irregular rusty red areas
